name: cloud-delegation
description: Linear-as-queue + cloud-agent (Codex, Cursor) delegation workflow — issue creation, polling, push-back-vs-fix matrix, agent env constraints, AGENTS.md auto-sync hook. v1.9: linear-workflow skill restructure — consolidate Codex/Cursor delegation flows, add explicit Status Transitions section, rename + clarify Code-Only PRs + Required Acceptance Criteria, drop redundant marker/MCP-tool subsections, scope note distinguishing Linear's first-party @Linear agent from third-party cloud agents. Substantial slimming (~530 lines removed, ~450 added) without behavior changes. v1.8: capture cartouche batching learnings — eligibility filter order (Codex-suspended → [CSR], per-agent env constraints, sibling-repo blockers), batch sizing rules (2+1+1 splits, pause-for-/compact, bundle-only-on-shared-module-tier-shape, parallelism gated on file-scope-non-overlap), honest-gap discipline (4 paths when queue drains to zero — wait/pivot LOCAL/cross-repo/review-mode — never silently backfill from non-eligible tasks). v1.7: add bundled-code-revisions bookkeeping variant — when a cloud-agent PR is mostly-good but ships small dead/unwanted code AND the agent's harness can't self-validate the fix (Cursor dialyzer OOM, Codex no-hex.pm, no-Tidewave anywhere), reviewer merges-as-is and bundles code revisions into the canonical commit-review Step 14–16 post-merge bookkeeping commit (single commit naming PR #N + INE-M for traceability), trading evaluator-separation for round-trip-cost savings. Documents preserved/lost trade-offs vs. canonical doc-only flow + four when-NOT-to-use guards. v1.6: retire don't-push hedge in favor of merge-train mode (`flow-review`) — workflow-only batch processor for 2+ open cloud-agent PRs that classifies by tier, dependency-sorts the queue, and runs the rebase cascade after each user-confirmed merge under the new mechanical-only rebase carve-out in delegation-rules.md. Also adds pre-flight conflict detection on N≥2 batch issue creation (touch-matrix on `## Files to modify` blocks → bundle / serializer / local / parallel recommendation), preventing cartouche-style fan-out where 4 of 9 issues touched the same registry file.
